answer: They will go to the beach
reasoning: Ka = future, haere = go, rātou = they, ki = to, tātahi = beach
Kua = perfect tense, patu = hit, au = I, i = object marker, te pōro = the ball

answer: The house was built by Bob
reasoning: I = past tense, hangaia = built (passive), te whare = the house, e = by, Bob = Bob

answer: The boy spoke to the girl
reasoning: Nā = emphatic marker, te tama = the boy, te kōtiro = the girl, i = marker, kōrero = speak

answer: The bull will chase the boys
reasoning: Mā = emphatic future, te pūru = the bull, ngā tama = the boys, e = marker, whai = chase

answer: I did not go to the courtyard
reasoning: Kāore = not, au = I, i = past marker in negative, haere = go, ki = to, te marae = the courtyard

answer: Kua moe rātou ki te hui
reasoning: Kua = have/has, moe = sleep, rātou = they, ki = at, te hui = the meeting

answer: Ka haere koe ki Te Maioro Nui Whakaharahara o Haina
reasoning: Ka = will, haere = go, koe = you, ki = to, Te Maioro... = Great Wall of China

answer: Kāore au e moe ki tātahi
reasoning: Kāore = not, au = I, e = negated future marker, moe = sleep, ki = at, tātahi = beach

answer: Kua horoia te pūru e koe
reasoning: Kua = has been, horoia = washed (passive), te pūru = the bull, e = by, koe = you

answer: Nā te tama te mahi i mahi
reasoning: Nā = emphatic marker, te tama = the boy, te mahi = the work, i = tense marker, mahi = do

answer: I haere te wahine ki te whare
reasoning: I = past tense, haere = go/went, te wahine = the woman, ki = to, te whare = the house

answer: Māori uses verb-subject-object word order. Tense/aspect markers appear at the beginning: Ka (future), Kua (perfect), I (past). Passive voice is formed by adding -a/-ia/-tia to the verb. The agent in passive constructions is marked with 'e'. Object marking uses 'i'. Emphatic constructions use Nā (past/present) or Mā (future) to emphasize the agent. Negation uses Kāore with appropriate tense markers.
reasoning: This explanation summarizes the grammatical patterns observed in the example sentences
